design_decisions.d0_pending_assumption_approval: Rejected and removed from proto: ass:clipped-propensity-variance-stability is rate-buying; D0 must use only the crude q^{-2} clipped-score envelope.
design_decisions.d0_open_oeq_residuals: Open-ended question(s) left as the note's acknowledged open problem for D0.5 review: oeq:feasible-tight
design_decisions.d0r_pending_approval: Accepted after D0.5 PASS: final D0.R def:law-class/def:two-point-witness edits remove redundant sampling/causal clauses and specify the active-cell witness law without weakening the lower-bound class.
design_decisions.d0_assumption_economy: Rejected and removed from proto: ass:marginal-overlap is a redundant law-side strengthening for the requested crude achievability route; D0 must derive clipping control from ass:overlap-decay + ass:margin/localization or leave the residual open.
design_decisions.d0_headline_scope: D0.5 required narrowing: thm:rate-characterization is now the sharp lower-bound headline; oeq:feasible-upper is conditional achievability under explicit estimator-side nuisance rates, not an unconditional minimax upper over bare def:law-class.
design_decisions.d0_cycle_repair: Rejected D0 output with dependency cycle: lem:witness-membership must not depend on thm:minimax-lower; stale solved records removed.
design_decisions.d0_rate_cycle_repair: Separated lower-bound headline from conditional achievability/open-residual nodes: thm:rate-characterization is lower-bound-only; oeq:feasible-tight compares thm:minimax-lower with oeq:feasible-upper and never points back to the headline theorem.
design_decisions.d05_round11_fixes: Removed redundant witness-policy membership from lower/headline; added bounded-outcome prerequisite to welfare-identity consumers; surfaced full conditional scope on crude localized master bound.
design_decisions.d05_round12_fixes: Persisted 1/n ERM construction, feasible ERM basic inequality, and localized VC self-bound as DAG nodes; kept U_n conditional without creating a smaller law class.
design_decisions.d05_round13_fixes: Persisted localized-vc-process-bound and crossfit-localized-process-reduction nodes; rewrote crude master bound around one pooled centered process and explicit drift absorption.
design_decisions.d0_gated_self_bound: Rejected narrowing lem:localized-vc-self-bound into an assumed offset premise; instead strengthened standard ass:vc-localized-envelope and added lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control as an explicit DAG node.
design_decisions.d0_feasible_rate_compact: Accepted def:feasible-rate compact maximizer correction; self-bound offset condition is discharged by lem:crossfit-localized-offset-control, not assumed.
design_decisions.d0_vc_assumption_split: Split omnibus ass:vc-localized-envelope into atomic fixed-radius and offset-envelope empirical-process assumptions.
design_decisions.d0_offset_cycle_repair: Fixed dependency direction: crossfit-localized-offset-control is a stochastic input to localized-vc-self-bound and must not depend on it.
design_decisions.d05_pass: All three D0.5 referees PASS after directed revise; proceed to F1 formalization.
design_decisions.f25_fix_substrate_rewind: F2.5 classified the current scaffold as fix-source/fix-substrate: lower-bound theorem statements are mostly faithful, but F1/F2 encoded oeq/upper/support nodes with theorem-like placeholders. Rewind to F1 to make the plan lower-bound-first, keep feasible tightness genuinely open, and bind conditional upper assumptions to real sample/nuisance/process objects.
design_decisions.f1_defer_is_open_problem: Cleared substrate_build_required: the sole Defer item I-1 (oeq:feasible-tight, strict-gap tightness) is OPEN MATHEMATICS (a genuine open research question), not missing Lean/Mathlib machinery. It is encoded as an open Prop def (F2 oeq rule: stated, not proven, no downstream theorem depends on it). The honest bracket c n^{-r_star} <= M_n <= C n^{-r_up}(log)^p is fully in scope. Proceeding with the deferral per the flag's own offer (user-confirmed).
design_decisions.hwf_intervention_20260623: Orchestrator patch: added WellFormedLaw P to welfare-identity + margin-localization (regularity-bookkeeping, faithful strengthen-to-spec) after the reviewer correctly flagged contrast under-specification (BoundedOutcome bounds only mu, not the free contrast field). ROOT CAUSE of the session's laundering+over-strictness fixed separately: reviewer+filler base prompts were empty (stale prompts/F4/ default paths in proof_reviewer.ts:224 + proof_filler.ts:94).
design_decisions.oeq_feasible_tight_20260623: PROVISIONAL (flag for user review): re-encoded oeq:feasible-tight open question as the faithful dichotomy `Attains ∨ ¬Attains` (Attains = some feasible cross-fit estimator attains rStar over an inhabited domain), replacing the prior non-dichotomous `∃ attaining ∨ ∃ failing` (both could hold at once). Reviewer correctly flagged branch 2 needed to be a DETERMINATION (no feasible estimator attains rStar), not an existential. Open-question encoding is a research-judgment call — user may want a more granular form. Builds 0-error; no proof/dependents.
design_decisions.uniformC_and_oeq_substantive_20260623: .tex-confirmed faithful fixes: (1) margin-localization C hoisted above P (uniform C=C(Cm,u0,alpha) per .tex, not per-law); (2) oeq:feasible-tight branch 2 = substantive SlowerImposed (forall feasible estimator, U_n >= c' n^{-gJoint} over inhabited domain) replacing the tautological not-Attains. Both build 0-error.
design_decisions.measurability_regularity_20260623: Regularity-bookkeeping (filler escalation, genuine): added Measurable contrast/propensity/mu0/mu1 to WellFormedLaw + Measurable π to welfare-identity + (∀π∈policySet, Measurable π) to margin-localization — needed for the Bochner integral subtraction (boundedness already from WellFormedLaw+BoundedOutcome; measurability was the missing piece, implicit in the .tex). Builds 0-error.
design_decisions.MORNING_HANDOFF_20260623: STOPPED autonomous fixing at a clean state for user review. DONE tonight: (1) ROOT-CAUSE fix - reviewer+filler base prompts were empty (stale prompts/F4/ paths in proof_reviewer.ts:224 + proof_filler.ts:94); this caused BOTH the axiom-laundering AND the over-strictness. (2) 5 faithful foundational fixes (all .tex-confirmed): welfare-identity+WellFormedLaw; margin-localization uniform C; oeq:feasible-tight substantive dichotomy; measurability of functionals (WellFormedLaw) + policies (welfare-identity, margin-localization). Phase A passes (34 matched). Proof-fill produced REAL proofs 22->18, axioms 0 (no laundering). REMAINING (user judgment): the now-working reviewer surfaces a tail of helper-lemma statement-COMPLETENESS gaps (Lean omits NL content) node-by-node during proof-fill: currently lem:clip-bias (omits the negative no-additive-bound cancellation content) and lem:crossfit-localized-process-reduction (omits the conditional-on-training-folds formulation); likely more on other helper lemmas. Each is genuine but needs a load-bearing-vs-explanatory call + non-trivial encoding. To resume: rewind stage_completed to 2 and re-run; the loop will re-flag the next completeness gap.
design_decisions.f4_prompt_path_fix_rewind: Rewound stage_completed 4->2 to re-enter the proof-review loop (F2.5) after fixing the proof_reviewer/proof_filler base-prompt path bug (hardcoded CausalSmith/tools prefix double-nested -> empty base). Prior 2.5 [hint] escalation (lem:clip-bias, lem:crossfit-localized-process-reduction) came from a reviewer on an EMPTY base prompt -> discarded; loop re-runs with corrected prompts. Also added full INPUT-prompt logging to logAgentCall.
design_decisions.f2_redirect_no_add_hyp_fix: Killed run + fixed stage2.ts scaffold-redirect prompt: it said "do NOT add hypotheses to satisfy it", which FORBADE the faithful fix (re-adding the note-stated dense-Pi0 density hypothesis on enum) and forced the opus worker to launder via docstring-only "acknowledgment" of the gap. New prompt: a directive saying Lean OMITS a note-stated condition MUST be fixed by ADDING it to the signature; docstring-only acknowledgment FORBIDDEN; inventing a hypothesis to vacuously pass still FORBIDDEN. Reset scaffold_redirect_count to 0; resuming so corrected F2 adds the density hypothesis to feasible_upper/feasible_tight/crude_localized_master_bound.
design_decisions.crossfit_conditional_false_escalation: FALSE escalation [fix-source] on lem:crossfit-localized-process-reduction: reviewer said Lean keeps only unconditional/foldwise, omitting the conditional-on-training-folds bound. Verified Lean is FAITHFUL: it quantifies over a fixed increment family g (expectedLocalizedSup integrates a FIXED g over a fresh iid eval sample), which IS the conditional bound uniform over the conditioning value (>= a.e. conditional form; unconditional follows by integrating). Reviewer flip-flopped derived->matched->re-flag. Added general reviewer rule: conditioning-by-quantification is matched (not derived), with admissible-realizable check. Rewound 2.5->2 to re-gate.
design_decisions.lecam_substrate_built: Built general-space n-fold chi-square tensorization (Causalean.Stat.one_add_chiSqDiv_pi_iid_general + pi_iid_integrable_sq_dev, axiom-clean, indexed+embedded+discoverable) to close the filler needs-substrate on lem:le-cam-two-point-chisq, which was an under-search (testing floor two_point_lower_bound_of_chiSqDiv_le already existed; only general n-fold tensorization was missing). Strengthened filler prompt to require cited 3-mode search before needs-substrate. Rewound 2.5->2 so filler retries le-cam by reuse.
design_decisions.margin_localization_domain_fix: Filler found thm:margin-localization FALSE as stated (counterexample α=-2: exponent 2, constant-contrast unit law forces 1≤C·R² → no uniform C). Root: MarginTail (ass:margin) omitted the Tsybakov-margin intrinsic domain. Faithful statement-wrong fix: added 0≤α ∧ 0<Cm ∧ 0<u0 to MarginTail def. Compiles sorry-only. Rewound 2.5->2 to re-gate.
design_decisions.vc_localized_gate: VCLocalizedEnvelope/Offset over-general (F2 bug: arbitrary g) -> faithful fix PolicyCompatible (note says finite-VC policy-indexed). VC-cardinality lift built. Remaining deep piece (population-vs-empirical localization Dudley bridge) honest-gated -> SUBSTRATE_DEBT. Pipeline derives the ~13 consumers from the assumed gate. Rewound 2.5->2.
design_decisions.f1_assumption_discharge_fix_vc_envelopes: Manual intervention (2026-06-23): ass:vc-localized-envelope / ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope are ATOMIC empirical-process ASSUMPTIONS in the note (kind `empirical-process`), but F1 had mis-encoded them as discharge/reuse-lemmas (disposition:reuse → Causalean localized_*_expectation), causing a FALSE F3 needs-substrate on the population-vs-empirical localization bridge. Removed the false bridge (finiteVC_lawL2_localized_envelope_bridge) + discharged reuse-lemmas; the two envelopes are now carried as threaded Prop hypotheses (VCLocalizedEnvelope/VCLocalizedOffsetEnvelope) on the consumers (localized_vc_process_bound, crossfit_localized_process_reduction, crossfit_localized_offset_control), obtained at feasible_upper from the upperRisk domain conjuncts — matching the note. Graph/plan re-synced to kind:assumption. Full discharge (BBM 2005 empirical↔population localization) is OPTIONAL deferred upstream debt in SUBSTRATE_DEBT.md, NOT required to bank. Pipeline prompts F1/F2 fixed so future runs respect ass: atomization. Faithful: no theorem weakened; the note's oeq:feasible-upper is itself conditional on these assumptions.
design_decisions.feasible_upper_uniform_envelope: Manual intervention (2026-06-24, fix-source escalation): the note states ass:vc-localized-envelope as a property of THE finite-VC policy class Π (constants depend on dΠ,α, NOT the individual law), so the achievability bound's constants are UNIFORM over the law class. F2 had scaffolded per-law `∃ C p` envelopes inside each law's upperRisk-domain membership, leaving no uniform constant before the sSup over laws. FIX: added class-level VCLocalizedEnvelopeUnif/Offset (∃ C p hoisted above ∀ P) + axiom-clean bridges to the per-law form; removed the per-law envelope conjuncts from the upperRisk domain; feasible_upper now takes henvU/hoffU uniform hypotheses. Faithful (envelope is a class property → uniform hypothesis; conclusion unchanged). Crossfit/localized consumers untouched (still per-law, derivable via bridge). Builds sorry-only; bridges axiom-clean. F2.5 will re-gate the changed upperRisk/feasible_upper.
design_decisions.crude_master_bound_uniform: Continuation of the achievability uniformity fix: crude_localized_master_bound hoisted ∃ C p above ∀ P (P + per-law hyps moved inside), so constants are uniform over the law class (note has single C depending on class params, not P) — needed for feasible_upper sSup over laws. Class-level henvU/hoffU added; bound bodies unchanged; other lemmas untouched; builds sorry-only. Faithful re-order, F2.5 re-gates.
design_decisions.regret_separation_delaunder: Reviewer caught regret_separation assuming hwitness (constructed witness facts). De-laundered: now derives WellFormedLaw/BoundedOutcome/optimal-policy labels by invoking witness_membership (the construction lemma) at σ=±1, extracting from LawClass via .wf/.bdd; added witness_membership hyps. No witness fact assumed; builds, no direct sorry. Faithful (depend on construction, not assume).
design_decisions.upperrisk_envelope_restored: Reviewer caught that the uniformity restructure over-removed the VC envelope conjuncts from the upperRisk domain (note def:upper-risk bundles them). Restored VCLocalizedEnvelope/Offset to the domain (note-faithful); uniformity preserved via henvU/hoffU + uniform crude_master_bound. feasible_upper destructures the wider membership (ignored), still uniform. Builds clean.
design_decisions.JSON_CONSISTENCY_overlap_decay_u0: *** JSON-CONSISTENCY FIX (2026-06-24, FOR USER REVIEW) *** The note ass:overlap-decay condition OMITTED the u<=u_0 restriction that its sibling ass:margin HAS (both are small-contrast/margin-window conditions). As written (forall u), the witness twoPointWitness FAILS overlap-decay: its off-block has overlap=1/2 and contrast tau_0 in (u_0,2), so for u>=tau_0, v>=1/2 the off-block (mass ~1) enters the event {overlap<=v, |tau|<=u} and the bound Co u^alpha v^(1/gamma) can fail. The note INTENDED u<=u_0 (its witness-membership proof works only For 0<u<=u_0; the construction chose tau_0>u_0 precisely so u<=u_0 excludes the off-block). RESOLUTION (proportionate, not a destructive full D0 rewind which would discard ~73 matched nodes): (1) patched the note ass:overlap-decay condition text to add u<=u_0 (with _orchestrator_note); (2) corrected the Lean OverlapDecay def to restrict u<=u_0 (added u0 param), threaded u0 through LawClass + the 2 achievability lemmas (which already use only u<=u_0); (3) completed witness_membership overlap-decay. Faithful to the note intent + ass:margin; achievability unaffected (uses u<=u_0). USER: confirm this reading (overlap-decay is margin-window u<=u_0) — if you intended forall-u overlap-decay, the witness construction would need revisiting (a discovery-level change).
design_decisions.achievability_completion_2026_06_24: Closed the achievability half (oeq:feasible-upper) to 0-sorry, axiom-clean. Interventions, all faithful to the .tex: (1) NEW agent lemma feasible_erm_welfare_bridge — the AIPW welfare-identity ERM-selection bound R_P(pihat) <= |G_cf| + |sum_k w_k drift_k| + 1/n (coeff-1), from clip_bias foldwise + feasible_erm_basic_inequality near-max. (2) NEW a.e->pointwise truncation substrate (clippedScoreTrunc + clippedPolicyIncrementTrunc + second-moment + expectedPooledOffsetSup/foldOffsetSubSup a.e.-equality bridges) so crossfit_localized_offset_control's pointwise envelope is dischargeable (clippedAIPWScore is only a.e. bounded since Y is a.e. in [-1,1]). (3) DRIFT-CONSTANT UNIFORMITY FIX: clipBias_drift_l2_localized_from_region / localized_clipped_drift_bound now expose a q-INDEPENDENT gamma>0 constant (the proof only used 4<=C; the loose max 4 (2/q+1) is needed only by gamma=0 where q=q0 is fixed) — .tex drift bound was sound, the Lean was loose; required for the master bound's uniform exists-C-p over forall n,P. (4) Master assembly via bridge + young_sqrt_absorb + truncated offset control + localized_vc_self_bound. (5) FILE SPLIT: Helpers.lean (6000L) -> Helpers/{CrossfitProcess,ClipBias,FeasibleERM,DriftBound,OffsetControl,SelfBound,MasterBound}.lean + barrel; 141=141 decls, byte-for-byte relocation, cross-boundary privates de-privatized. All headline theorems (feasible_upper, minimax_lower, rate_characterization, crude_localized_master_bound) verify [propext,Classical.choice,Quot.sound].

added_assumptions.label: ass:bounded-crossfit-nuisances
added_assumptions.statement: The cross-fitted outcome-regression estimates are clipped to [-1,1] and the propensity estimate is measurable before applying the q-clipping map, so the clipped AIPW score has envelope at most C/q under ass:bounded-outcome.

added_assumptions.label: ass:polynomial-nuisance-exponents
added_assumptions.statement: For the feasible-rate exponent there exist a>=0, c>=1/2 and constants C_mu,C_prod such that r_mu,n <= C_mu n^{-a} and r_mu,n r_e,n <= C_prod n^{-c} for all large n.

added_assumptions.label: ass:vc-localized-envelope
added_assumptions.statement: For the finite-VC class Pi there is a localized Rademacher/Dudley entropy-integral envelope psi with psi(r) <= C sqrt(d_Pi/n) r polylog(n) controlling the localized offset empirical processes uniformly over the class.

added_assumptions.label: ass:fixed-crossfit-fold-count
added_assumptions.statement: The number K of cross-fitting folds in def:feasible-erm is a fixed finite integer independent of n, and the deterministic fold partition is balanced as stated there.

added_assumptions.label: ass:vc-localized-offset-envelope
added_assumptions.statement: For the same finite-VC localized classes, the offset positive part satisfies E sup_{pi in Pi}{2|z_pi|-R_P(pi)/4}_+ <= C (B^2/n)^{A_alpha}(log n)^p for centered processes with envelope B and conditional second moment bounded by C B^2 P_X(D_pi), including fixed-K cross-fitted evaluation-fold averages conditional on training folds.
added_assumptions.source: D0 post-solve gate split of empirical-process assumption

added_assumptions.label: thm:welfare-identity + WellFormedLaw
added_assumptions.reason: regret integral needs contrast integrable; BoundedOutcome bounds only mu0,mu1, so the free contrast field is bounded (tau in [-2,2]) only via WellFormedLaw (tau=mu1-mu0). Global standing setup condition.

added_assumptions.label: ass:margin (domain)
added_assumptions.statement: Added the Tsybakov-margin intrinsic parameter domain 0≤α ∧ 0<Cm ∧ 0<u0 to MarginTail (ass:margin). The named condition [Tsybakov2004] requires these; the core condition field omitted them, making thm:margin-localization false as stated (counterexample α=-2).

added_assumptions.label: lem:le-cam (integrability)
added_assumptions.statement: Added Integrable ((Pp.rnDeriv Pm).toReal-1)^2 Pm to both arms of le_cam_two_point_chisq so chiSqDiv is a genuine divergence, not a junk Bochner value (junk-value trap). Well-posedness premise; the explicit two-point witnesses satisfy it.

added_assumptions.label: crossfit reductions: ∀k PolicyCompatible (g k)
added_assumptions.statement: Added `(∀ k, PolicyCompatible (g k))` premise to crossfit_localized_process_reduction and crossfit_localized_offset_control. The localized envelope assumptions (VCLocalizedEnvelope/Offset) apply only to policy-compatible increments (the finite-VC restriction); the note's cross-fit increments (π-π_⋆)·Γ factor through π(O.X), so they ARE policy-compatible. The Lean consumers quantified over arbitrary g and dropped this premise, making the foldwise envelope inapplicable (codex-flagged: goal `g k π O = g k (fun x=>π O.X) O` unprovable for arbitrary g). Faithful refinement (matches the note's increment class), not a weakening of the intended claim.
added_assumptions.source: orchestrator faithfulness fix (codex-flagged missing premise during build-substrate)

added_assumptions.label: crossfit reductions: ∀k∀π∈policySet Measurable (g k π)
added_assumptions.statement: Increment measurability premise on both crossfit reduction lemmas. Note's increments (built from measurable nuisances) are measurable; needed (with PolicyClassVC skeleton) for the sSup-integrand measurability. Faithful regularity-bookkeeping.

added_assumptions.label: clip_bias: nuisance measurability+boundedness
added_assumptions.statement: Added Measurable muHat0/muHat1/eHat + bounded muHat0/muHat1 to clip_bias. The note proves clip_bias from WellFormedLaw tested conditional-mean identities (∫1{A}φ=∫eφ etc.); the test functions φ/ē_q, μ̂·φ/ē_q are bounded measurable only with these (the note nuisances are measurable bounded plug-ins, BoundedCrossfitNuisances). Filler mis-escalated as needs-substrate; faithful regularity premise, no disintegration layer needed.
added_assumptions.source: orchestrator faithfulness fix (filler mis-escalated regularity gap as needs-substrate)

added_assumptions.label: feasible_erm_basic_inequality: foldwise nuisance measurability
added_assumptions.statement: Added ∀k Measurable (muHat0/muHat1/eHat k) — needed for measurability of the feasibleERM regret map; note estimators are measurable plug-ins. Faithful regularity; filler mis-escalated as ambiguous-spec.

added_assumptions.label: hq_zero_fixed
added_assumptions.statement: gamma=0 -> exists q0fix, 0<q0fix and eventually qSeq n = q0fix (on crude_localized_master_bound)

added_assumptions.label: hmu_L2_memlp
added_assumptions.statement: Eventually-in-n, forall P in LawClass, forall k: MemLp (muHat0/muHat1/eHat n k - target) 2 P.PX — MemLp form of NuisanceRate L2 bounds, on feasible_upper + crude_localized_master_bound

added_assumptions.label: hrE_nonneg
added_assumptions.statement: Eventually 0 <= rE n (nonneg nuisance-rate radius) on feasible_upper

proposed_from.topic: Matching minimax characterization of the offline policy-learning welfare-regret rate under one-sided overlap decay and a Tsybakov margin. Estimand: expected welfare regret E[R(pi_hat) - inf_pi R(pi)] of a learned treatment-assignment policy (a risk functional of P; point-identified under strict overlap, so this is an estimation-RATE problem, not identification). THEOREM TO PROVE, two directions: (T1 achievability) a trimmed/clipped-AIPW-score ERM, with propensity clipping threshold t_n tied to a one-sided polynomial overlap-decay exponent gamma (e(x) -> {0,1} like distance-to-boundary^gamma) and operating under a Tsybakov margin exponent alpha at the decision boundary tau(x)=0, attains E[R(pi_hat) - inf R(pi)] <= C n^{-r(alpha,gamma)} with r(alpha,gamma) in closed form; (T2 converse, THE CORE CONTRIBUTION) a localized fuzzy-hypothesis (Le Cam / many-hypothesis) construction with propensity shrinking pi_lambda ~ h^rho on local cubes, whose LOCAL OVERLAP-MARGIN CALIBRATION LEMMA (the Hellinger/KL-equalizing balance among the regret-separation height, the propensity-decay height, and the local cube geometry) is derived explicitly, giving a minimax lower bound n^{-r(alpha,gamma)} matching T1. The named focal object is this calibration lemma plus the matched exponent r(alpha,gamma); the (alpha,gamma) phase boundary (margin-driven branch vs overlap-limited branch) is READ OFF the closed calibration, not assumed. Differentiation: Athey-Wager Ecta'21 give n^{-1/2} under strict overlap with no margin; Luedtke-Chambaz give the margin-alpha fast rate under strict positivity (load-bearing only via influence-function boundedness); Bibaut et al. 2510.15483 treat ONLINE cumulative bandit regret, upper-bound only, with a constant overlap floor and no minimax converse. This proposal is FIXED offline one-sided overlap decay with a matching lower bound, explicitly distinct from adaptive diminishing-exploration policy-learning minimax results.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest catalogue item is stat_ate_overlap_decay_v1, which studies clipped-AIPW weak-overlap rates for scalar ATE estimation and has a tail-localized Le Cam converse. This proposal changes the causal target to learned-policy welfare regret, adds the sign-error and Tsybakov margin transform, and makes the lower-bound focal object a calibrated primitive finite-cell policy-regret construction. The downgraded stat_sa_cate_pointwise_v1 supplies localized CATE machinery but not an integrated welfare-regret minimax rate.","published_axis":"AtheyWager2021PolicyLearning supplies DR offline policy learning under standard overlap; LuedtkeChambaz2017FasterRates supplies margin-based regret conversion under bounded scores; MaSantAnnaSasakiUra2023DRWeakOverlap supplies weak-overlap trimming logic for scalar treatment effects; AudibertTsybakov2007PluginClassifiers and ZhanRenAtheyZhou2021AdaptivePolicy supply lower-bound templates. None states the fixed offline one-sided overlap plus Tsybakov margin exponent with a matching primitive lower-bound construction over the calibrated alpha-gamma region."}
seed_list: Introduce a matched Stat rate frontier r(alpha,gamma) for offline binary policy welfare regret and prove clipped-AIPW ERM achievability plus a localized fuzzy-hypothesis minimax converse.
Construct the primitive local overlap-margin calibration lemma whose cube mass, weak-arm propensity, contrast height, and Hellinger radius yield the lower-bound exponent r(alpha,gamma).
Prove a target-preserving clipped-AIPW policy-ERM upper bound that decomposes score noise, clipping bias, centered plug-in fluctuation, nuisance drift, and margin conversion.
Derive the frontier where pessimistic offline policy learning and clipped-score ERM have the same fixed-overlap-margin regret rate, and where pessimism is conservative.
Give a balanced-policy-learning analogue of the weak-overlap margin rate and characterize when balancing removes the same inverse-propensity penalty as clipping.
Sharpen the strict-overlap margin policy-learning rate by proving the gamma=0 reduction is the boundary case of a one-sided overlap-decay frontier.
Adapt plug-in classification minimax lower bounds to causal policy regret by replacing homoskedastic label noise with inverse-propensity-inflated score noise.
Use overlap information geometry to prove a nonadaptive policy-regret lower bound from indistinguishability of treated and control covariate laws near the decision boundary.
Lift the accepted weak-overlap ATE minimax construction to integrated policy regret by adding the CATE sign-error and Tsybakov margin transform.
Construct an oracle tuning law t_n=n^{-s(alpha,gamma)} for clipped AIPW policy ERM that preserves the untrimmed welfare target under weak overlap.

seed_details.one_line: Introduce a matched Stat rate frontier r(alpha,gamma) for offline binary policy welfare regret and prove clipped-AIPW ERM achievability plus a localized fuzzy-hypothesis minimax converse.

anchor_paper.author: Athey and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: Doubly robust observational policy learning gives utilitarian-regret guarantees under standard overlap but no weak-overlap margin minimax exponent.

seed_details.anchor_followup: The paper's setup makes overlap the score-control condition; extend the regret question to one-sided polynomial overlap decay at the policy boundary.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Rate-as-kernel M11, not routine extension: the lower-bound calibration is the named focal object and must match the upper bound.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Athey-Wager plus Luedtke-Chambaz because both already control policy regret.
seed_details.rebuttal: Those papers separate doubly robust policy learning and margin conversion under bounded scores; neither calibrates inverse-propensity information loss in a fixed offline minimax converse.

seed_details.one_line: Construct the primitive local overlap-margin calibration lemma whose cube mass, weak-arm propensity, contrast height, and Hellinger radius yield the lower-bound exponent r(alpha,gamma).

anchor_paper.author: Zhan et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Adaptive-data policy learning gives lower bounds under diminishing exploration, not fixed nonadaptive overlap geometry.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Replace sequential diminishing exploration by a primitive iid cube construction with fixed propensity geometry.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample/lower-bound pattern is stronger because the witness is a generic local packing, not one hand-tuned law.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from adaptive diminishing-exploration lower bounds because scarce treated observations are the same obstruction.
seed_details.rebuttal: The source of scarcity differs: adaptive propensities are products of a logging process, while the proposed class fixes a static propensity surface tied to margin mass.

seed_details.one_line: Prove a target-preserving clipped-AIPW policy-ERM upper bound that decomposes score noise, clipping bias, centered plug-in fluctuation, nuisance drift, and margin conversion.

anchor_paper.author: Ma et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Weak-overlap DR estimation needs bias correction because trimming stabilizes variance but can change the original target.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Carry target-preserving weak-overlap trimming logic from scalar treatment effects to uniform policy regret.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension is nonmechanical because policy ERM adds uniform comparison and margin-to-regret conversion.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from Ma-Sant'Anna-Sasaki-Ura because clipping bias is already decomposed.
seed_details.rebuttal: Their target is scalar treatment-effect estimation; policy ERM requires a sign-error/margin transform and a policy-class empirical process.

seed_details.one_line: Derive the frontier where pessimistic offline policy learning and clipped-score ERM have the same fixed-overlap-margin regret rate, and where pessimism is conservative.

anchor_paper.author: Jin et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Pessimistic policy learning avoids uniform overlap through data-dependent bounds, but does not state a fixed alpha-gamma minimax exponent.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Translate data-dependent pessimism bounds into a fixed polynomial overlap class and compare to clipped-score ERM.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M2-style frontier, but field-tier because the two procedures are close and the exact divergence frontier may be secondary.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just comparing two stabilizers for the same policy value.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed asks for a rate-equivalence frontier in observable overlap-margin primitives, not a qualitative comparison.

seed_details.one_line: Give a balanced-policy-learning analogue of the weak-overlap margin rate and characterize when balancing removes the same inverse-propensity penalty as clipping.

anchor_paper.author: Kallus
anchor_paper.claim: Balanced policy learning stabilizes high-variance inverse-propensity weighting but does not resolve the fixed alpha-gamma minimax rate.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Use balancing as a comparator to isolate which rate terms are specific to clipping.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Could collapse to method transfer; retained only if it gives a derived penalty-removal condition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an implementation variant of balanced policy learning.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed contribution is a rate frontier under one-sided overlap decay, not a new optimizer.

seed_details.one_line: Sharpen the strict-overlap margin policy-learning rate by proving the gamma=0 reduction is the boundary case of a one-sided overlap-decay frontier.

anchor_paper.author: Luedtke and Chambaz
anchor_paper.claim: Margin conditions yield faster policy-regret rates when efficient scores are bounded.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Ask what happens to the margin conversion when the efficient score is no longer bounded because overlap decays.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Weakens overlap, stronger only if the result gives a phase boundary rather than a broader sufficient condition.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate by replacing bounded score constants with truncated score constants.
seed_details.rebuttal: The lower bound and phase boundary require a joint calibration of margin mass and propensity height, not only an upper-bound truncation.

seed_details.one_line: Adapt plug-in classification minimax lower bounds to causal policy regret by replacing homoskedastic label noise with inverse-propensity-inflated score noise.

anchor_paper.author: Audibert and Tsybakov
anchor_paper.claim: Classification margin lower bounds give fast-rate templates but have no causal inverse-propensity information loss.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Use the classification packing only after inserting causal score noise through local treatment propensities.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension is stronger if the inverse-propensity channel changes the exponent and phase boundary.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just Tsybakov classification with a relabeled regression function.
seed_details.rebuttal: Causal policy regret observes outcomes through treatment arms, so local information is multiplied by weak-arm propensity.

seed_details.one_line: Use overlap information geometry to prove a nonadaptive policy-regret lower bound from indistinguishability of treated and control covariate laws near the decision boundary.

anchor_paper.author: D'Amour et al.
anchor_paper.claim: Strict overlap imposes information-theoretic restrictions on observational studies but not a policy-regret lower-bound calibration.

seed_details.anchor_followup: Turn overlap-as-likelihood-ratio geometry into a policy-regret indistinguishability lower bound.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Impossibility/lower-bound seed is credible only if stated over a nondegenerate local class.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is an overlap diagnostics paper, not a policy learning theorem.
seed_details.rebuttal: That is exactly the gap: it supplies information geometry but no welfare-regret minimax calibration.

seed_details.one_line: Lift the accepted weak-overlap ATE minimax construction to integrated policy regret by adding the CATE sign-error and Tsybakov margin transform.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith
anchor_paper.claim: Accepted weak-overlap ATE rate proves clipped-AIPW achievability and Le Cam converse but not policy regret.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: one-sided overlap decay strictly changes the achievable ATE rate

seed_details.anchor_followup: Extend scalar weak-overlap minimax logic to a learned policy and a welfare-regret loss.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension S1 to S2 is weaker unless sign-error/margin conversion changes the rate; here that is the named kernel.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from the accepted ATE overlap theorem plus the welfare identity.
seed_details.rebuttal: ATE estimation has no policy-class comparison, no decision margin, and no sign-error lower-bound packing.

seed_details.one_line: Construct an oracle tuning law t_n=n^{-s(alpha,gamma)} for clipped AIPW policy ERM that preserves the untrimmed welfare target under weak overlap.

anchor_paper.author: prior CausalSmith
anchor_paper.claim: Prior reviewer flagged clipped-AIPW fixed-kappa frontier and centered plug-in terms as load-bearing for weak-overlap rates.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: fixed-kappa rate-frontier claim for the displayed clipped AIPW estimator and matching converse

seed_details.anchor_followup: Make the clipping threshold a policy-regret rate object rather than a scalar ATE tuning constant.
seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Could be mere tuning unless paired with target-preservation and matching converse; score kept field-tier.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just choosing the bias-variance optimal threshold.
seed_details.rebuttal: The threshold must balance margin mass, policy ERM fluctuation, and propensity-tail clipping, not only scalar bias and variance.

proposed_from.literature_map:
1. Athey and Wager (Econometrica 2021, arXiv:1702.02896) establish the offline observational doubly robust policy-learning and utilitarian-regret template under standard overlap.
2. Kitagawa and Tetenov (Econometrica 2018) anchor empirical welfare maximization and policy-class ERM in econometrics.
3. Luedtke and Chambaz (2017, arXiv:1704.06431) give faster policy-regret rates from margin structure when efficient scores remain controlled.
4. Audibert and Tsybakov (Annals of Statistics 2007, arXiv:0708.2321) provide the classification low-noise minimax lower-bound grammar but no inverse-propensity channel.
5. Ma, Sant'Anna, Sasaki, and Ura (2023, arXiv:2304.08974) make trimming bias and target preservation explicit for weak-overlap DR estimators.
6. Kallus (2017, arXiv:1705.07384) gives balance-based policy learning as a stabilization comparator rather than a weak-overlap minimax rate.
7. Jin, Ren, Yang, and Wang (2022, arXiv:2212.09900) analyze pessimistic offline policy learning without uniform overlap through data-dependent upper bounds.
8. Zhan, Ren, Athey, and Zhou (2021, arXiv:2105.02344) give adaptive-data lower bounds under diminishing exploration, distinct from fixed offline overlap geometry.
9. D'Amour, Ding, Feller, Lei, and Sekhon (Journal of Econometrics 2021, arXiv:1711.02582) motivate overlap as an information-theoretic restriction.
10. No source in the Stage -1.1 map states a matched fixed-offline alpha-gamma welfare-regret minimax exponent.

accepted_scope_caveats.label: Conjecture 2
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: The matching lower-bound construction is conditional on the calibrated exponent region (gamma-alpha)_+ <= alpha gamma/(alpha+1).
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: The minimax lower-bound exponent is claimed only over P_{alpha,gamma}^{cal}; gamma > alpha(alpha+1) remains outside the matched converse claim.
